name: Produce blog drafts with Google Drive and WordPress at scale

messageValues.message: You write production-quality scheduling content. Follow the brief exactly, match the requested tone, and return clean markdown with a title line. No preamble, no explanations.
You are a strict editor. Check the draft for factual gaps, tone mismatches and weak openings. Return the improved draft only — do not describe your changes.

parameters.content:
### Hourly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 12 h. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Build the Brief

### Build the Brief

Shapes the item: writes `brief`, `tone`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Draft with AI Writer

### Draft with AI Writer

Single fixed LLM step (no tool choice) — cheaper and more predictable than an agent.

Next: Refine with AI Editor

### Anthropic Writer Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Draft with AI Writer** as `languageModel`

🔑 Anthropic API key

### Refine with AI Editor

Single fixed LLM step (no tool choice) — cheaper and more predictable than an agent.

Next: Publish Draft to WordPress

### Anthropic Editor Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Refine with AI Editor** as `languageModel`

🔑 Anthropic API key

### Publish Draft to WordPress

wordpress — `action` operation against the connected account.

Next: Cap Batch Volume

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 WordPress credentials

### Cap Batch Volume

Caps the stream at 100 items to protect downstream quotas.

# Produce blog drafts with Google Drive and WordPress at scale

**Pattern:** Content Generation · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Produce blog drafts with Google Drive and WordPress at scale. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual scheduling is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Hourly Schedule → Build the Brief → Draft with AI Writer → Refine with AI Editor → Publish Draft to WordPress → Cap Batch Volume

## Use it for
- Teams that want to content generation without manual effort
- Anyone automating scheduling in marketing
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Scales without extra headcount
- Responds in seconds
- Standardizes the process every time

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Anthropic API key; WordPress credentials.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your scheduling process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
